title: [FIRRTL] (>>> ^) mismatch
body:
The following FIRRTL program

```python
; seed: 7564
circuit top_mod :
  module top_mod :
    input inp_db: SInt<18>
    output _tmp53: UInt<18>
    wire tmp49: SInt<18>
    tmp49 <= dshr(inp_db, tail(asUInt(inp_db), 11))
    _tmp53 <= xor(tmp49, asSInt(UInt<13>(2856)))
```

Compiled with `firtool   --lower-to-hw --infer-widths --imconstprop --lowering-options=disallowPackedArrays,emittedLineLength=8192 --mlir-timing --verilog -o=$VFILE2` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(	// a_top_mod.fir:3:10
  input  [17:0] inp_db,
  output [17:0] _tmp53);

  assign _tmp53 = $signed(inp_db) >>> $signed(inp_db[6:0]) ^ 18'hB28;	// a_top_mod.fir:3:10, :7:{14,27}, :8:15
endmodule
```

Compiled with `firrtl-1.5-SNAPSHOT` produces this Verilog:

```verilog
module top_mod(
  input  [17:0] inp_db,
  output [17:0] _tmp53
);
  wire [17:0] _GEN_0 = inp_db;
  wire [17:0] tmp49 = $signed(inp_db) >>> _GEN_0[6:0];
  assign _tmp53 = $signed(tmp49) ^ 18'shb28;
endmodule
```

`Yosys 0.9+3911 (git sha1 4b31223e, clang 11.0.1 -fPIC -Os)` reports formal mismatch:

```
ERROR: Found 18 unproven $equiv cells in 'equiv_status -assert'.
```
